body_txt: Some people just can't resist the urge to look at themselves in the mirror. Not only can mirrors give you a perspective on what you look like , they can reveal the person you've become , or you might just end up learning something unexpected about yourself . However, some people just take the urge way too far. This trope refers to when a character is so obsessed with their own visage that they just can't look away from it. Not only is it hard for this character with this trope to get away from themself in the mirror and not resist the urge to make sure they're just right, but the very mirrors they center themselves around become iconic to them. Over the millennia, mirrors (especially their constant usage) have become a symbol of vanity, narcissism , and overall obsession with one's appearance because the literal self-reflection they provide can quickly become twisted into a desire for perfectionism , to the point that all that one can look at is oneself. Please note that this trope doesn't refer to any time a character looks at themself in the mirror. It's specifically about the connection between mirrors (and their usage) and vanity, narcissism, and/or overall appearance obsession. Even if a character is vain, if the usage of mirrors doesn't come to mind for them, or if a character uses mirrors frequently but isn't vain, it's likely that they are not an example. Related to House of Broken Mirrors and Rage Against the Reflection , where a broken mirror can become reflective of a broken ego . See also Clocks of Control for another household object used to represent a character's negative traits. This trope is why the Vain Sorceress is likely to have a Magic Mirror on the wall to proclaim then the Fairest of Them All . Not to be confused with literal vanity mirrors , though the pun was very much intended.
